question: TELL ME ABOUT REVERSE BOWLING
content:
Reverse bowling is a fundamental drill used to mechanize and stabilize the end of the approach. The bowler begins close to the foul line and gradually adds steps in reverse order.
Typical progression:

Start with a 1‑step (finish position only)
Then 2 steps
Then 3 steps
Build back toward the full approach

Because the bowler starts at the foul line, the drill removes momentum and speed variables, allowing full focus on:

Proper finish position
Balance at the line
Correct timing at release
Stable posture
Clean tempo

Reverse bowling makes it easier to feel when the ball should arrive at the line in relation to the slide foot.
What reverse bowling is used for
Reverse bowling helps:

Improve balance at the foul line
Fix early or late timing
Clean up release consistency
Stabilize tempo
Reduce drifting, falling off shots, or muscling the ball

Reverse bowling → focuses on the END of the approach
1‑2‑3 drill → focuses on the START of the approach (pushaway, pace, early timing)
